contentBlocks.original:
आ तू न॑ इन्द्र क्षु॒मन्त᳚-ञ्चि॒त्र-ङ्ग्रा॒भं स-ङ्गृ॑भाय ।
म॒हा॒ह॒स्ती दक्षि॑णेन ॥ 1 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
ā tū na̍ indra kṣu̠manta̎-ñchi̠tra-ṅgrā̠bhaṃ sa-ṅgṛ̍bhāya ।
ma̠hā̠ha̠stī dakṣi̍ṇēna ॥ 1 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: O Indra (here addressed as Ganapati, lord of hosts), come to us with abundant and wonderful wealth. O great-handed one, with your right hand gather these gifts for us.
We know you as the greatly powerful, the greatly bountiful, the greatly wealthy one, of immense greatness — may you come to us with your many protections.

contentBlocks.original:
न॒ हि त्वा᳚ शूर दे॒वा न मर्ता᳚सो॒ दित्स᳚न्तम् ।
भी॒म-न्न गां-वाँ॒रय᳚न्ते ॥ 3 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
na̠ hi tvā̎ śūra dē̠vā na martā̎sō̠ ditsa̎ntam ।
bhī̠ma-nna gāṃ vā̠raya̎ntē ॥ 3 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Neither the gods nor mortals can restrain you, O hero, when you are willing to bestow. None can obstruct the flow of gifts from the awe-inspiring one.

contentBlocks.original:
एतो॒न्विन्द्रं॒ स्तवा॒मेशा᳚नं॒-वँस्वः॑ स्व॒राजम्᳚ ।
न राध॑सा मर्धिषन्नः ॥ 4 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
ētō̠nvindra̠ṃ stavā̠mēśā̎na̠ṃ vasva̍-ssva̠rājam̎ ।
na rādha̍sā mardhiṣannaḥ ॥ 4 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Come, let us praise Indra (Ganapati), the sovereign lord of riches, the self-ruling one. He will not abandon us or deprive us of his generous bounty.
May he send forth his praise-worthy gift and draw near as the Sama hymn is sung in his honor. May he approach us, attracted by our offerings.

contentBlocks.original:
आ नो᳚ भर॒ दक्षि॑णेना॒भि स॒व्येन॒ प्र मृ॑श ।
इन्द्र॒ मानो॒ वसो॒र्निर्भा᳚क् ॥ 6 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
ā nō̎ bhara̠ dakṣi̍ṇēnā̠bhi sa̠vyēna̠ pra mṛ̍śa ।
indra̠ mānō̠ vasō̠rnirbhā̎k ॥ 6 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Bring us gifts with your right hand, O Ganapati, and touch us with your left. O Indra (lord of wealth), do not deprive us of your treasures.

contentBlocks.original:
उप॑क्रम॒स्वा भ॑र धृष॒ता धृ॑ष्णो॒ जना᳚नाम् ।
अदा᳚शूष्टरस्य॒ वेदः॑ ॥ 7 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
upa̍krama̠svā bha̍ra dhṛṣa̠tā dhṛ̍ṣṇō̠ janā̎nām ।
adā̎śūṣṭarasya̠ vēda̍ḥ ॥ 7 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Come forward boldly and bring wealth to the people, O bold one. Take what belongs to those who are uncharitable and bestow it on the deserving.

contentBlocks.original:
इन्द्र॒ य उ॒ नु ते॒ अस्ति॒ वाजो॒ विप्रे᳚भि॒-स्सनि॑त्वः ।
अ॒स्माभि॒-स्सुतं स॑नुहि ॥ 8 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
indra̠ ya u̠ nu tē̠ asti̠ vājō̠ viprē̎bhi̠-ssani̍tvaḥ ।
a̠smābhi̠-ssutaṃ sa̍nuhi ॥ 8 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: O Ganapati, whatever prize of yours is to be won by the wise — grant that boon to us who bring our offerings and press the Soma.

contentBlocks.original:
स॒द्यो॒जुव॑स्ते॒ वाजा᳚ अ॒स्मभ्यं᳚-विँ॒श्वश्च᳚न्द्राः ।
वशै᳚श्च म॒क्षू ज॑रन्ते ॥ 9 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
sa̠dyō̠juva̍stē̠ vājā̎ a̠smabhya̎ṃ vi̠śvaścha̎ndrāḥ ।
vaśai̎ścha ma̠kṣū ja̍rantē ॥ 9 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Swiftly come to us your prizes, all-delightful and gladdening. May they hasten to us at your divine command.

contentBlocks.original:
ग॒णाना᳚-न्त्वा ग॒णप॑तिं हवामहे
क॒वि-ङ्क॑वी॒नामु॑प॒मश्र॑वस्तमम् ।
ज्ये॒ष्ठ॒राज॒-म्ब्रह्म॑णा-म्ब्रह्मणस्पत॒
आ नः॑ शृ॒ण्वन्नू॒तिभि॑स्सीद॒ साद॑नम् ॥ 10 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
ga̠ṇānā̎-ntvā ga̠ṇapa̍tiṃ havāmahē
ka̠vi-ṅka̍vī̠nāmu̍pa̠maśra̍vastamam ।
jyē̠ṣṭha̠rāja̠-mbrahma̍ṇā-mbrahmaṇaspata̠
ā na̍-śśṛ̠ṇvannū̠tibhi̍ssīda̠ sāda̍nam ॥ 10 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: We invoke you, O Ganapati — lord of the ganas (hosts), the wisest among the wise, the most renowned of seers, the eldest king of Brahman. O Brahmanaspati, hearing our invocations, come and be seated in your sacred place and bless us with your divine protections.

contentBlocks.original:
नि षु सी᳚द गणपते ग॒णेषु॒ त्वामा᳚हु॒र्विप्र॑तम-ङ्कवी॒नाम् ।
न ऋ॒ते त्वत्क्रि॑यते॒ कि-ञ्च॒नारे म॒हाम॒र्क-म्म॑घवञ्चि॒त्रम॑र्च ॥ 11 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
ni ṣu sī̎da gaṇapatē ga̠ṇēṣu̠ tvāmā̎hu̠rvipra̍tama-ṅkavī̠nām ।
na ṛ̠tē tvatkri̍yatē̠ ki-ñcha̠nārē ma̠hāma̠rka-mma̍ghavañchi̠trama̍rcha ॥ 11 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Sit among your ganas, O Ganapati — you are hailed as the most learned among the wise. Nothing is accomplished without your grace, O far-reaching one. O magnanimous lord, worship the great and wondrous divinity that you are.

contentBlocks.original:
अ॒भि॒ख्यानो᳚ मघव॒न्नाध॑माना॒न्त्सखे᳚ बो॒धि व॑सुपते॒ सखी᳚नाम् ।
रणं᳚ कृधि रणकृत्सत्यशु॒ष्माभ॑क्ते चि॒दा भ॑जा रा॒ये अ॒स्मान् ॥ 12 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
a̠bhi̠khyānō̎ maghava̠nnādha̍mānā̠ntsakhē̎ bō̠dhi va̍supatē̠ sakhī̎nām ।
raṇaṃ̎ kṛdhi raṇakṛtsatyaśu̠ṣmābha̍ktē chi̠dā bha̍jā rā̠yē a̠smān ॥ 12 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: O renowned and generous Ganapati, be a friend to those who implore you, O lord of riches — a friend among friends. O true-spirited one, grant us inner strength and valor. Bestow your abundant wealth upon your devotees.
